task_completion.description: Log into OrientDB Studio and access the demodb database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OrientDB Studio web interface (typically at localhost:2480) with the 'demodb' database selected and active.

task_completion.description: Identify asymmetric edges and shared hotel stays for the cohort.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of SQL queries, graph views, or browse results checking for shared stays at 'The Savoy' or 'Hotel de Crillon' among the cohort users (john.smith, david.jones, emma.white, maria.garcia, sophie.martin).

task_completion.description: Create the justified reverse HasFriend edges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the execution of 'CREATE EDGE HasFriend' commands or graph UI interactions specifically creating edges: david.jones@example.com -> john.smith@example.com, david.jones@example.com -> emma.white@example.com, and sophie.martin@example.com -> maria.garcia@example.com.

task_completion.description: Materialize the parallel TravelAffinity edge layer for the justified pairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the creation of a 'TravelAffinity' edge class and/or the insertion of 'TravelAffinity' edges between the same justified user pairs (david->john, david->emma, sophie->maria).

task_completion.description: Tag the TravelAffinity edges with the required rule version.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence in the query editor or record inspector showing that the newly created TravelAffinity edges have a property (e.g., RuleVersion) explicitly set to 'v2026q1'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OrientDB Studio web interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions are performed within the browser showing the OrientDB Studio UI, not via a standalone script or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands (like curl or python scripts) to bypass the required GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not the primary tool used for executing the graph database modifications.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the database records rather than just typing the expected output into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show OrientDB Studio success messages, updated graph visualizations, or query results confirming the edges were actually created in the database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Cohort members: john.smith@example.com, david.jones@example.com, emma.white@example.com, maria.garcia@example.com, sophie.martin@example.com. Expected reverse HasFriend edges to be created: david.jones@example.com -> john.smith@example.com, david.jones@example.com -> emma.white@example.com, and sophie.martin@example.com -> maria.garcia@example.com. These are justified by shared stays at 'The Savoy' (London) and 'Hotel de Crillon' (Paris). The TravelAffinity edges must be created for these same pairs and must include a property (e.g., RuleVersion) set exactly to 'v2026q1'.
